geographic_flags.quote: East Malaysia and rural Peninsular lag significantly
geographic_flags.why_biased: Groups East Malaysia with rural Peninsular into single 'lagging' category, treats Klang Valley as universal benchmark
geographic_flags.fix: Acknowledge digital growth in East Malaysian urban centres

economic_flags.why_biased: Emotive vulnerability framing advocates specific policy outcome
economic_flags.fix: Frame as logistical challenge of dual payment infrastructure

gender_flags.why_biased: Total Gender Erasure. Male-centric default agency implied
gender_flags.fix: Incorporate gender-disaggregated data on adoption and victimisation

omission_flags: Informal economy role (pasar malam, street vendors) sustaining cash usage omitted
No mention of NSRC or institutional accountability for fraud spike
evasion_flags: Avoids naming specific e-wallet providers with poor security records, instead highlighting 'social engineering' which shifts responsibility to victims
shoe_test_failures: Swapping rural for urban breaks logic — assumes geography is primary barrier, ignores urban exclusion of elderly and B40
overall_assessment: Partisan-neutral but displays significant gender erasure and factual inconsistency. Uses outdated 2023/24 DuitNow QR figures (1.2B vs reported 3B for 2025), creating misleading transaction-to-fraud ratio. The 68% figure likely conflates segment share with total retail.
